response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine staff-level breadth — high-throughput microservices at Sociomantic handling millions of RPS, consistent agile and TDD discipline, and recent hands-on MCP/agentic tooling work at re:cinq that maps directly to the AI-native engineering context. However, Python is entirely absent from his resume and skills list despite being a stated must-have, his distributed-systems depth dates primarily to 2018–2020, and his AI-native exposure spans only six months before a current caregiving gap — making the overall profile a conditional fit that warrants a conversation before a firm call.
strengths: Demonstrated staff-level technical ownership across multiple organizations, with direct high-throughput microservices experience (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O) at Sociomantic
Recent hands-on contribution to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq, directly relevant to an AI-native engineering baseline
Strong and consistent software-craftsmanship discipline — TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD — practiced across multiple roles and reinforced through public workshops and teaching
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume, skills list, and all employment history despite being an explicit must-have; the stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang throughout
Distributed-systems experience at the required depth dates to 2018–2020 (Sociomantic); more recent roles are primarily product-layer TypeScript and tooling work
Current career gap combined with a six-month tenure at re:cinq raises questions about operational continuity and readiness to immediately carry an on-call rotation
